sections.title: Fats
sections.narration: 0.3g of fat. omega 3 is doing a lot of the work here. for a vegetable, nutrient payoff is the whole point.
sections.subtitleText: 0.3g of fat. omega 3 is doing a lot of the work here. for a vegetable, nutrient payoff is the whole point.

sections.title: Carbs
sections.narration: 6g of carbs. the sugar load matters more than you would want. for vegetables, low downside is useful, but there still needs to be real payoff.
sections.subtitleText: 6g of carbs. the sugar load matters more than you would want. for vegetables, low downside is useful, but there still needs to be real payoff.

sections.title: Proteins
sections.narration: 1g of protein. bioavailability is one of the best parts of the protein story. protein is not the main pitch here, but extra support still matters.
sections.subtitleText: 1g of protein. bioavailability is one of the best parts of the protein story. protein is not the main pitch here, but extra support still matters.

sections.title: Vitamins
sections.narration: vitamin c at 140% daily value, vitamin a at 35% daily value stand out most. that is major vitamin support.
sections.subtitleText: vitamin c at 140% daily value, vitamin a at 35% daily value stand out most. that is major vitamin support.

sections.title: Minerals
sections.narration: minerals are basically not part of the case here.
sections.subtitleText: minerals are basically not part of the case here.

sections.title: Pros
sections.narration: on the plus side: easy to build meals around. a practical plus because good foods need realistic use cases. helps add volume without much calorie cost. that makes vegetables easier to use often. very low downside whole food. vegetables win a lot simply by offering clean nutritional payoff.

sections.title: Cons
sections.narration: the drawbacks next: freshness and prep can matter. practical friction is still a real downside. rarely carries a meal by itself. that is normal here, but still part of the tradeoff. usually needs pairing to make a full meal. most vegetables are not complete by themselves.

closing.summary: Red Bell Pepper has a genuinely strong overall case. the practical downsides are real, but they do not outweigh the upside. best when you want low downside and easy micronutrient support.
closing.useCaseNote: Best when you want low downside and easy micronutrient support.
closing.cta: Would you rank it the same, or nah?

explanation.summary: Fats are carrying this food most, while minerals are the biggest drag. It lands in A tier.
